plugins.name: zaksak
plugins.description: A collection of Claude Code utilities by Zak Sakata

plugins.name: spec-ops
plugins.description: Spec workflow (write → refine → launch → verify): write-spec is the workflow entrypoint that turns a bare idea into a spec — eliciting and distilling requirements (full rigor) before drafting concise feature specs at a requested rigor (light AC-only → full deep spec) that open with a flat, id'd Acceptance Criteria contract (optionally organized into ordered named groups); refine-spec hardens a draft into an implementation-ready spec, commits the grounded group order, and hunts unstated constraints; launch-spec compiles the ready spec into a self-contained /goal driver gated on every acceptance criterion (emit-only), phasing a large or hard-sequenced build by AC group; verify-spec checks the implementation matches the spec's claims and every acceptance criterion against real code, git, and live state — with the evidence standard scaled to each criterion and a verification method recorded per AC — and flags delivered code that maps to no criterion (backward coverage); on a spec re-run it drifts against the last clean verification — re-grounding only criteria whose evidence moved and flagging regressions. spec-ops also commits the spec artifact at each stage — write-spec the draft, refine-spec the implementation-ready spec (Stop-hook-enforced), launch-spec bakes a per-phase commit cadence into the /goal driver — each commit scoped to the spec file (never a broad add) and never pushed.

plugins.name: ralph
plugins.description: Ralph Loop — autonomous agentic coding workflow with sandbox isolation, scoped memory, and self-managing task iteration

plugins.name: skill-manager
plugins.description: Manage Claude Code skills from inside any project: configure which plugins a project uses, publish/sync local skills to a central marketplace, remove them, and diagnose and auto-repair the setup. Adapts to any repo layout; authoring is delegated to the native skill-creator skill.

plugins.name: worktree-ops
plugins.description: Git worktree workflow for parallel Claude Code sessions, built on native worktree support, with deterministic enforcement (a skill-scoped PreToolUse guard, active only while the pull/merge skills run, blocks squash merges and committing unresolved conflicts; helper scripts make pre-flight, conflict listing, and merge-confirm repeatable): create-worktree spins up an isolated worktree and flips the session into it (fresh from origin, .worktreeinclude env files copied, optional issue/PR seed, idempotently keeps .claude/worktrees/ gitignored, opt-in setup script); list-worktrees shows a dashboard of every worktree (ahead/behind, dirty, PR, current marked); pull-worktree rebases/merges a worktree onto a chosen base branch with an ask-first conflict policy; merge-worktree commits, pushes, opens/merges the PR into a chosen target (never squashed) only after confirming MERGED, runs an opt-in teardown script, then removes the worktree and returns to main.

plugins.name: pm-ops
plugins.description: PM intake-to-implementation workflow (consolidate → draft → route → promote) that bookends spec-ops; markdown-in-git is the source of truth and the board is a projection. Everything is a spec — one format, scaled rigor: spec-ops:write-spec authors every spec body (short for trivial items, full for complex), and full rigor additionally runs spec-ops:refine-spec; threaded by a stable PM-#### id. consolidate-backlog turns an unstructured dump into deduped, classified, sized candidate items; draft-spec scopes one candidate into a spec at the right rigor and moves it backlog/ → specs/draft/; route-task projects the spec onto a board through a pluggable, board-agnostic engine (GitHub Issues + Projects ships first), dry-by-default then writes the board ref back; promote-spec moves a verified spec into its implementation repo's docs/specs/ and leaves a forwarding stub behind. A deterministic core (lib/pm.py: ids, scaffolding, front-matter I/O, normalization, index) plus an engine contract keep decision logic out of prose and boards swappable by a one-line config change.
